title: Persona Attribution Fixture — allowlisted row schema (v1)
description: Authoritative allowlist for tests/fixtures/persona-attribution/*.jsonl rows — narrow excerpts of parent-session JSONL kept in the public repo for spike replay. additionalProperties: false at every level is the privacy gate enforced by tests/test-allowlist.sh (A10). Single-purpose script scripts/redact-persona-attribution-fixture.py is the ONLY supported producer. Any field not enumerated here is rejected. Fixture filenames MUST follow gate-<gate>-persona-<persona>-<seq>.jsonl — no UUIDs, no project names. Spec: docs/specs/token-economics/spec.md (v4.2 §Privacy). Security design: docs/specs/token-economics/plan/raw/security.md (O1).

type.description: Structural marker — fixtures here are already-extracted dispatch summaries (one row per Agent invocation), NOT verbatim session JSONL rows. Pinned to a single literal so the schema rejects accidental verbatim copies of session rows (which have type assistant/user/system/etc and would carry prompt content).

agentId.description: 17-hex linkage ID emitted by Claude Code in the parent's tool_result trailing text (verified empirically across multiple production sessions). Opaque; no PII.

tool_use_id.description: Opaque tool-use ID emitted by Claude Code; used for joining assistant→tool_use→tool_result rows in spike replay.

parent_session_uuid.description: UUID of the parent ~/.claude/projects/<proj>/<uuid>.jsonl session. Allowed inside the row; the FIXTURE FILENAME must NOT use this UUID — it must use gate-<gate>-persona-<persona>-<seq>.jsonl.

model.description: Public model name (e.g. claude-opus-4-7). No version-private names.

usage.description: Token counts only; no prompt/response text.

tool_uses.description: Count of tool uses inside the subagent — pure int.

total_tokens.description: Annotation total; A1.5 reconciles against sum(usage.*) in the canonical_token_source path.

persona_path.description: Repo-relative path to the persona file. Persona directories are 'review' (for the spec-review gate), 'plan', and 'check' — note the dir name 'review' deliberately differs from the gate name 'spec-review'. MUST point only inside the public personas/ tree — no absolute paths, no adopter project paths.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC truncated to the HOUR (minutes and seconds always 00). Strips work-pattern signal per security O3 / spec §Privacy.
